e2e.summary: 12 passed / 6 failed across 6 viewports x 3 browsers (chromium, firefox, webkit). Failures isolated to chromium only due to SVG <circle> cx/cy parse errors (empty/NaN coordinates).

recommendations: Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers ~11KB
Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers (saves ~150ms)

issues: hero shows 'Application error: a client-side exception has occurred' - app crashes (major)
all other sections render as unstyled HTML fallback (no CSS, no canvas, no theme) (major)
missing ProjectVisuals canvas/svg core (major)
no HUD frame, no orange/dark theme applied (major)
hero renders blank white canvas with only grid lines - main visual missing (major)
about/skills/projects/experience/contact all show unstyled HTML fallback (major)
no CSS or canvas/svg core loaded (major)
complete loss of design system at desktop breakpoint (major)
hero renders blank white canvas with only grid lines - main visual missing (major)
all sections render as unstyled HTML fallback (major)
no CSS or canvas/svg core loaded (major)
complete loss of design system at large desktop breakpoint (major)
contact section title 'OPENING // THE_CORE' wraps awkwardly producing 'ING // THE_CO RE' (major text break)
contact section 'OPEN' prefix appears clipped above viewport when scrolled to anchor
contact title still wraps as 'ING // THE_COR E' due to large font on narrow column (major)
skills HUD bottom: 'LAT 37.77°N / LON -122.42°W' overlaps 'OPENAI API' text row (minor)
contact title 'OPENING // THE_CORE' partially obscured by '06' badge overlapping 'OPEN' (major)
experience section second card placeholder shows 'CORE // ONLINE' overlap with HUD frame (minor)

instructions: Convergence rule: lhDesktop.performance >= targetDesktopPerf AND lhMobile.performance >= targetMobilePerf AND e2e.failed === 0 AND visual.overallScore >= 80.
If E2E failed > 0, list the specific viewport+browser combos that failed and a concrete fix per failure.
If lhDesktop or lhMobile under target, list the top 5 LH opportunities (e.g., "reduce unused JS", "minimize main-thread work") with file paths if known.
If visual issues exist, list them with viewport + section + suggested fix.
Return JSON { converged:bool, breakdown:{desktopPerf, mobilePerf, e2eRate, visual}, blockedBy:string[], recommendations:[{priority, category, file?, action}], summary:"..." }
prompt.role: QA lead synthesizing perf + responsive scores
prompt.task: Decide if we have converged. If not, produce a prioritized recommendation list for the next iteration.
